name: Ria Money Transfer Agent
description_short: Ria Money Transfer is an international remittance service operating in 190+ countries with over 600,000 payout locations, offering multiple ways to send and receive money globally.
description_long:
Ria Money Transfer has operated for over 35 years as a money transfer service, building a track record in international remittances and cross-border payments. The company positions itself as a fast, secure alternative for sending money to family and friends worldwide, with particular strength in emerging markets and regions with large diaspora populations.

Ria's core service is international money transfers available through multiple channels: online via website, mobile app, in-person at agent locations, and via WhatsApp. Senders can choose from multiple payment methods (debit card) and receivers have flexibility with bank deposits, cash pickup at 600,000+ locations, digital wallets, home delivery, and ATM withdrawals. The platform supports over 100 currencies and operates in 190+ countries, with particular emphasis on routes to Mexico, Latin America, Africa, Asia, and Europe.

Ria differentiates itself through distribution breadth (600,000+ payout locations), multi-channel sending options, transparency on exchange rates and fees displayed upfront, and technology integrations like WhatsApp transfers and mobile app tracking. The company claims to have processed over one billion transfers and maintains relatively high app ratings (4.9/5 on iOS, 4.5/5 on Trustpilot). They also market promotional rates on first transfers and sponsor the Mexican National Team.

As a money transfer agent network rather than a bank, Ria's primary limitation is that it does not offer deposit accounts, lending, or credit services. Fees are transaction-based and rates fluctuate with currency markets. Users must have access to participating agent locations or digital payment methods to send funds, and the service is optimized for remittances rather than domestic transactions. The company's regulatory framework varies by country and jurisdiction.
address: Mundo Latino Multiservice, 105 Clinton St, New York, NY 10002

company_info.headquarters: Mundo Latino Multiservice, 105 Clinton St, New York, NY 10002

pros: Operates in 190+ countries with over 600,000 payout locations, providing extensive global coverage
Multiple send methods including online, mobile app, in-person, and WhatsApp integration for convenience
Transparent pricing displayed upfront with promotional rates available on first transfers
Fast transfers with real-time tracking available through mobile app
Multiple receive options including cash pickup, bank deposit, digital wallet, ATM withdrawal, and home delivery
Supports over 100 currencies for flexible international transactions
35+ years operational history with claim of over 1 billion transfers processed
cons: Transaction fees apply (example shows $1.99 USD fee on sample transfer) in addition to exchange rate margins
Exchange rates not guaranteed and fluctuate based on market conditions and time of transfer
Limited to remittance use case—does not offer deposit accounts, lending, or credit-building services
Requires access to participating agent locations or digital payment methods for sending
Regulatory compliance and service availability vary significantly by country and jurisdiction
best_for: International workers and immigrants sending remittances to family in home countries
Individuals with frequent cross-border payment needs to 190+ countries
Users preferring multiple send/receive options including cash pickup and mobile app convenience
Businesses and individuals seeking established provider with 35+ year track record in global transfers
diagnosis: Ria Money Transfer is best for immigrants and international workers who need to send money frequently to family in multiple countries and prefer access to extensive cash pickup networks and app-based convenience. The main caveat is that this is purely a remittance service with no banking, lending, or credit products—it functions as a money movement platform, not a financial institution.
services: Mobile app money transfer with tracking
In-person cash payment at agent locations
